EF1 TORNADO CONFIRMED BY NWS CHARLESTON STORM SURVEY. TOUCHED DOWN ALONG HWY 78 WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 2.2 MILES AND A PATH WIDTH OF ABOUT 40 YARDS. DAMAGED 2 MOBILE HOMES...MOVING ONE HOME 5 FT OFF ITS FOUNDATION AND PRODUCED SIGNIFICANT ROOF/WINDOW/DOOR DAMAGE TO THE OTHER HOME. MOVED A VEHICLE 50 FT.

*** 1 INJ *** EF2 TORNADO CONFIRMED BY NWS CHARLESTON STORM SURVEY. TOUCHED DOWN IN WESTERN ALLENDALE COUNTY WITH A PATH LENGTH 9.5 MILES AND A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF ABOUT 3/4 MILE. ESTIMATED SPEEDS 115-125 MPH. DAMAGE INCLUDES UPROOTED/SNAPPED 1500-2000 TREES...TWO MOBILE HOMES DESTROYED...CELL PHONE TOWER TOPPLED...MINOR TO MODERATE ROOF/SIDING DAMAGE TO OVER 2 DOZEN HOMES...MANY POWER LINES/POLES KNOCKED DOWN...SEVERAL DOZEN VEHICLES DAMAGED AND AN EMPTY SEMI TRAILER OVERTURNED. INJURY TO MOTORIST.

EF2 TORNADO CONFIRMED BY NWS CHARLESTON STORM SURVEY. PATH LENGTH OF 0.5 MILES AND MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 100 YARDS. ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS OF 120-130 MPH. TORNADO CAUSED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO GEORGIA POWER MCINTOSH PLANT. 1 WATER COOLING RESERVOIR TOWER BUILDING DESTROYED...ANOTHER SUFFERED MODERATE DAMAGE. TWO HIGH VOLTAGE TOWERS TOPPLED. 5 SEMI TRAILERS TOSSED BETWEEN 300-700 YARDS...WITH ONE CONTAINER UNACCOUNTED FOR. STEEL HOIST/ROOF ASSEMBLY WEIGHING 1 TON TOSSED ABOUT 100 YARDS.

INJURY TO MOTORIST.

*** 1 INJ *** LIBERTY COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED 17 HOMES HAVE SUSTAINED MODERATE TO SEVERE DAMAGE IN THE LAKE GEORGE SUBDIVISION JUST EAST OF LIMERICK GEORGIA. SEVERAL HOMES HAVE BEEN CONDEMNED DUE TO THE EXTENSIVE NATURE OF THE DAMAGE. NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND UPROOTED. ONE INJURY OCCURRED WHEN A TREE FEEL INTO A HOME. PRELIMINARY INDICATIONS FROM THE EMERGENCY MANAGER IS THAT THE DAMAGE WAS DUE TO DOWNBURST WITH WINDS ESTIMATED TO BE IN EXCESS OF 80 MPH.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS DOWNED NUMEROUS TREE LIMBS AND POWER LINES IN MIDWAY.

*** 1 INJ *** LIBERTY COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS WIDESPREAD WIND DAMAGE IN THE LAKE GEORGE COMMUNITY. NUMEROUS OAK TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND UPROOTED. MANY STRUCTURES DAMAGED AND ROOFS HAVE BEEN COMPLETELY BLOWN OFF SEVERAL HOMES. ONE INJURY OCCURRED WHEN A TREE FEEL IN A HOME. A PRELIMINARY ASSESSMENT BY THE EMERGENCY MANAGER IS ONGOING BUT PRELIMINARY FINDINGS SUGGEST THIS DAMAGE WAS CAUSED BY A DOWNBURST.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE HAS COMPLETED A DAMAGE SURVEY OF CHATHAM COUNTY GEORGIA AREA AND HAS DETERMINED THAT AN EF-1 TORNADO OCCURRED. THE TORNADO DEVELOPED AROUND 337 PM JUST NORTH OF THE INTERSECTION OF MONTGOMERY CROSS ROAD THE HARVEY TRUMAN PARKWAY AND TRAVELED SOUTH-SOUTHWEST JUST WEST OF THE HARVEY TRUMAN PARKWAY FOR ABOUT 3/4 OF MILE BEFORE LIFTING. THE MAXIMUM WIDTH OF THE TORNADO WAS ABOUT 100 YARDS. THIS TORNADO DAMAGED A FIRE STATION AND UPROOTED AND SNAPPED OFF NUMEROUS TREES.

*** 1 INJ *** THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE HAS COMPLETED A DAMAGE SURVEY IN BERKELEY COUNTY AND HAS DETERMINED THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH 70 TO 80 MPH WINDS OCCURRED. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF GROOMSTOWN ROAD AND ENDLESS LANE...THEN MOVED NORTHWARD ALONG GROOMSTOWN ROAD. PATH WAS 1/3 MILE LONG AND 75 YARDS WIDE. 4 MOBILE HOMES SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE. ONE LARGE TREE AND ONE SMALL TREE UPROOTED. MANY LIMBS SNAPPED OFF OF TREES. A LARGE TREE SPLIT NEAR THE BOTTOM OF THE TRUNK. ONE POWER POLE SNAPPED OFF. ONE LARGE CHICKEN COOP LIFTED OFF THE GROUND AND MOVED 6 TO 8 INCHES. ONE LIGHTWEIGHT ALUMINUM SHED BLOWN 150 FEET AND DESTROYED. ONE CAR WAS PUSHED 25 TO 30 FEET BY THE WIND. SEVERAL SMALL WOODEN PUMP HOUSES APPROXIMATELY 6 FEET BY 6 FEET WERE DESTROYED. BASEBALL SIZED HAIL. 1 MINOR INJURY.

NWS SURVEY TEAM HAS DETERMINED THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO OCCURRED IN THE JOHNS ISLAND AREA SUNDAY AFTERNOON. INITIAL TOUCHDOWN OCCURRED JUST WEST OF CHISOLM ROAD VERY NEAR ANGLE OAK ELEMENTARY SCHOOL. THE TORNADO CROSSED CHISOLM ROAD AND PORTIONS OF POINT PARK ROAD BEFORE LIFTING JUST BEHIND SAINT JOHNS HIGH SCHOOL NEAR CHURCH POINT LANE. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE 60-70 MPH.

TREES BLOWN DOWN ON POINT PARK ROAD JUST SOUTH OF CHISOLM ROAD. TOPS OF SEVERAL TREES SHEARED OFF. POSSIBLE DAMAGE TO GARAGE. NWS SURVEY TEAM WILL INVESTIGATE THE DAMAGE MONDAY MORNING TO DETERMINE WHETHER A TORNADO OCCURRED.
